headlines.title: Soaring Imports Push India’s Crude Stocks to Near 1-Year High
headlines.summary: India’s strategic and commercial crude oil inventories have jumped to a nearly one-year high as the world’s third-largest crude oil buyer boosted its imports to a record high in June. As at the end of June, India’s crude oil stocks held in strategic, commercial, and refinery stor

headlines.title: Citi: Oil Could Sink to $60 as Hormuz Traffic Normalizes
headlines.summary: Brent Crude prices could plunge to as low as $60 per barrel by the end of the year, says Citigroup, which expects flows through the Strait of Hormuz to soon normalize and the U.S. and Iran to reach a deal in the coming months. “We continue to recommend selling any summer rallies

headlines.title: India Denies Directly Exporting Fuel to Russia, But Admits Traders May Be
headlines.summary: Indian refiners are not directly exporting any refined petroleum products to fuel-starved Russia, although some supplies from traders are likely reaching Russia, India’s Oil Minister, Hardeep Singh Puri, has said. Reports emerged earlier this week that Russia had started importin

headlines.title: Oil Set for Fourth Straight Weekly Loss as Hormuz Flows Return
headlines.summary: Oil futures were on track to post their fourth consecutive weekly loss early on Friday as the tentative reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the uptick in oil flows weigh down prices. In Asian trade early on Friday, with U.S. markets closed for the July 4 weekend, both benchmark

headlines.title: Japan's LNG Imports Fall 7% as Utilities Chase Cheaper Coal
headlines.summary: Japan last month reduced gas-fired generation in favor of coal as the price of liquefied natural gas remained elevated despite multiple reports of energy flows out of the Strait of Hormuz improving. headlines.title: UAE Rewrites Offshore Oil Pricing To Capture Asian Markets
headlines.summary: Previously, we reported that Abu Dhabi’s flagship crude, Murban crude, has rapidly risen to prominence, with Murban crude futures having rapidly evolved from a regional benchmark into a primary global pricing standard. headlines.title: Tehran Is Racing the Clock to Export as Much Oil as Possible
headlines.summary: Iran is wasting no time in returning full steam to the oil export market after the Islamic Republic and the United States agreed in mid-June on a 60-day window to negotiate a deal. Since the memorandum of understanding was signed and the U.S. blockade aimed at preventing Iranian

headlines.title: Europe’s Energy Transition Stalls as Heat Wave Exposes the Cost of Inaction
